name: Diego
presentation: Social anxiety (eating in public / speaking to others)
data_condition: sparse (4 entries) + MISSING DT on some entries
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional. Tests handling of missing fear-thermometer values.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Diego says he doesn't eat lunch at school because people might watch him eat. He comes home starving.
entries.parent_response: I started packing foods he can hide in his bag and eat in the bathroom.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Diego asked me to make the call, said he'd say something wrong.
Diego wouldn't tell the coach he'd been given the wrong size jersey, he just took it.
entries.parent_response: I emailed the coach for him.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Diego didn't answer 'here' loud enough and got marked absent, and was too embarrassed to correct it.
entries.parent_response: I emailed the school to fix the attendance.

situations: Being observed doing routine acts (eating in public)
Speaking to adults/authority figures and on the phone
parental_responses: Emailing adults to handle things for him
safety_behaviors: Avoidance of eating at school (eating in the bathroom)
Getting a parent to speak/call for him
Not correcting errors / staying under the radar
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct. Two of four entries have no DT — check the report still produces sensible situation scores (and doesn't invent ratings).
